title: Jessie Buckley's Critics Choice Awards 2026 Win
text: Jessie Buckley won Best Actress at the 2026 Critics Choice Awards for her role as Agnes Shakespeare in the film Hamnet, directed by Chloé Zhao. In an exclusive E! News interview backstage, the 36-year-old actress expressed her gratitude for the recognition. Buckley explained that being in a room with accomplished artists she admires was the most meaningful aspect of the experience. She described the source material—Maggie O'Farrell's novel—as transformative, saying: When I read the book it kind of ripped through me like a hurricane...it feels pretty sexy. The actress praised director Zhao's artistic vision, noting that she communicated that compelling feeling onto screen. Despite the film's heavy subject matter about Shakespeare's grief over losing his son, Buckley emphasized that filming was enjoyable. She recalled: It was fun every day...Chloé keeps a very light set and at the end of every week do these dance takes where we'd blast Rihanna. In her acceptance speech, Buckley expressed affection for her co-star Paul Mescal, stating: I could drink you like water working with you every single day. You are a giant of the heart.
